summary: CC-0088 delivers a kind-only Gateway API quick-start path that exposes Keystone at https://keystone.127-0-0-1.nip.io/v3 via Envoy Gateway + EnvoyProxy NodePort + nip.io wildcard DNS, backed by two chainsaw suites, seven shell-unit tests, a production-posture guard, a Renovate customManager, and documentation updates. All blocker criteria pass: deploy/flux-system/ is byte-identical to origin/main; the gateway-quick-start Keystone suite drives the real Envoy Gateway controller end-to-end with no manual HTTPRoute status patching; the smoke suite asserts HTTP 200 with a non-empty JSON body containing `version`; both chainsaw suites carry `overlay: kind` labels plus runtime presence probes (belt-and-braces gating, mirrors flux-web-health); wait_for_gateway_programmed dumps describe + envoy-gateway-system logs on timeout; renovate.json pins the chart version with `minimumReleaseAge: "3 days"`, majors disabled, automerge on minor/patch; Quick Start's Access section is rewritten with the nip.io endpoint as the primary flow and kubectl port-forward moved to a Fallback subsection; Step 7 CR gains `spec.gateway` with `openstack-gw` / `keystone.127-0-0-1.nip.io` / `/`. A handful of minor issues remain: two `# Reviewer: please verify` leftover comments in envoy-gateway.yaml, a potential HTTPRoute-conflict race when the smoke and keystone-suite CRs attach the same hostname+path concurrently, an internal kubectl-wait timeout (8m) that exceeds the outer chainsaw script timeout (7m) in the smoke suite, and the unauthenticated `oci://docker.io/envoyproxy` HelmRepository which can hit Docker Hub rate limits. None are blockers.

tests_checklist.notes: Seven shell-unit tests covering port-mapping shape, gateway-wait happy/timeout paths, production posture (git-diff guard against origin/main), renovate customManager regex capture, Step 2b table row + architecture snapshot, Step 7 sample-CR structural yq assertions, access-section rewrite, reference-docs cross-links. Two chainsaw suites: keystone/gateway-quick-start drives the real Envoy Gateway controller through Gateway Programmed → listener Accepted → Certificate Ready → NodePort 31443 verification → HTTPRoute Accepted (no patch) → Keystone HTTPRouteReady + status.endpoint, and infrastructure/gateway-quick-start-smoke asserts HTTP 200 with non-empty JSON body containing `version` from the CI host. Both suites use overlay label + runtime presence-probe gating, both carry catch blocks with describe + podLogs. Shell tests gate on optional tooling (yq, jq, npx, perl, git) with SKIP counters.

code_quality_checklist.notes: All new/changed files carry SPDX Apache-2.0 headers and cite CC-0088 + REQ-*. Shell scripts follow the established PASS/FAIL/SKIP-counter pattern + `source tests/lib/assertions.sh` convention. Chainsaw suites mirror the flux-web-health belt-and-braces gating pattern. Manifests carry extensive DECISION comments explaining chart-source and NodePort-pinning choices. Two leftover `# Reviewer: please verify` notes in deploy/kind/base/envoy-gateway.yaml (lines 36, 47) duplicate the review-pattern flag from CC-0065; they should be resolved or rewritten as declarative DECISION comments before merge.

security_checklist.notes: Self-signed cert matches the OpenBao UI posture (documented kind-only demo). `listenAddress: 127.0.0.1` on the extraPortMappings keeps the endpoint local-only — it is explicitly not bindable on the host's public interface. Gateway/openstack-gw lives in the `openstack` namespace (same-namespace parentRef → no ReferenceGrant needed, matches the operator's stated scope at keystone_types.go:348-354). curl `-k` usage is inside the smoke test only and is clearly documented as kind-only. No secrets logged in stubs/fixtures. The unauthenticated `oci://docker.io/envoyproxy` HelmRepository can be rate-limited by Docker Hub on high-churn CI; not a security issue per se but a supply-chain availability concern.

architecture_checklist.notes: Production overlay (deploy/flux-system/) is untouched — verified by tests/unit/deploy/production_posture_test.sh comparing against origin/main. Kind-only addons (Envoy Gateway controller + GatewayClass + Certificate + Gateway) live under deploy/kind/base/ exclusively. REQ-012 operator-platform-agnostic invariant preserved. Gateway choice left to customers in production, per docs/reference/keystone-crd.md:316-318 (the kind-only admonition in keystone-crd.md makes this explicit). HelmRepository + HelmRelease inlined in envoy-gateway.yaml mirrors headlamp.yaml precedent; the header DECISION block explains why this was chosen over adding a shared source under deploy/flux-system/sources/.

dry_yagni_checklist.notes: No speculative fields added to the CR schema; spec.gateway was already delivered by CC-0065. No new bash helpers beyond wait_for_gateway_programmed, which directly mirrors wait_for_fluxinstance's diagnostic shape. Chainsaw suite naming (`keystone-gateway-qs-api`, `keystone-smoke-api`) follows the operator's existing `{cr-name}-api` HTTPRoute suffix convention. Minor: the duplicated `keystone-smoke` and `keystone-gateway-qs` CR fixtures in two separate suites are justified by parallel-execution isolation of database names — but they also create an HTTPRoute-conflict race on the shared openstack-gw listener with the same hostname+path (see issues_found).

fail_fast_checklist.notes: wait_for_gateway_programmed exits non-zero on timeout with describe + podLogs dumps (unit-tested on happy + timeout paths). Chainsaw catch blocks dump describe + podLogs on every step. Production-posture shell test FAILs if deploy/flux-system/ drifts from origin/main. Smoke test FAILs on HTTP != 200, empty body, or missing `version` field. Runtime presence probe in both chainsaw suites exits 0 with a SKIP line when fixtures are absent — intentional belt-and-braces for operators who forget the `overlay!=kind` selector.

defensive_checklist.notes: Shell tests bound `|| true` usage to diagnostic dumps and optional-tooling probes where SKIP is the right answer (git worktree detection, yq/jq/npx/perl availability). The JSONPatch on EnvoyProxy targets /spec/ports/0/nodePort by index rather than by name — DECISION comment justifies the choice against name-drift across chart versions. Gateway listener uses `allowedRoutes.namespaces.from: Same` — correct minimum surface for the same-namespace Keystone CR without a ReferenceGrant.

architecture_findings.title: Leftover `# Reviewer: please verify` comments in envoy-gateway.yaml
architecture_findings.description: Two DECISION blocks end with `# Reviewer: please verify this matches intent.` and `# Reviewer: please verify against the pinned chart version.` These are review-time prompts, not durable documentation, and will rot in the repo once the PR is merged. Matches the review pattern from CC-0065 that flagged similar leftover reviewer notes.
architecture_findings.suggested_fix: Resolve the ambiguity in the text itself (e.g., drop the prompt and state the decision as finalised), or remove the two lines outright. The surrounding DECISION/Reason/Chose paragraphs already carry the full rationale.

issues_found.title: HTTPRoute hostname+path collision between gateway-quick-start and smoke suites
issues_found.description: Both CRs attach to `parentRef.name: openstack-gw`, `hostname: keystone.127-0-0-1.nip.io`, `path: /`. Under chainsaw's `parallel: 4` the operator will synthesize two HTTPRoutes (`keystone-gateway-qs-api` + `keystone-smoke-api`) that overlap entirely on hostname+path, which the Gateway API spec allows but marks one as Accepted=False, reason RouteRuleConflict. Whichever suite wins first is non-deterministic; the loser's Keystone CR never flips HTTPRouteReady=True and times out. The database-name isolation comment in 01-smoke-keystone-cr.yaml:13-21 acknowledges the parallelism but not this routing conflict.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Either (a) differentiate paths (e.g., smoke uses `/v3`, keystone-suite uses `/`), or (b) apply `chainsaw-config.yaml` `parallel: 1` to these two suites via separate config, or (c) document a `--selector` split so infra-smoke and keystone-suite are not expected to run simultaneously in CI. Option (a) is cleanest — smoke suite only curls `/v3` anyway.

issues_found.title: Inner kubectl-wait timeout (8m) exceeds outer chainsaw script timeout (7m) in smoke suite
issues_found.description: `timeouts.exec: 7m` and `timeout: 7m` on the script bound the whole step at 7m, but the script contains `kubectl wait --for=condition=HTTPRouteReady keystone/keystone-smoke --timeout=8m`. Chainsaw will kill the script at 7m regardless, so the 8m inner bound can never fire — the only visible failure mode is chainsaw's generic script-timeout kill, not a targeted `HTTPRouteReady not reached` diagnostic.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Either raise `timeouts.exec`/`script.timeout` to ≥ 9m (kubectl-wait 8m + Gateway Programmed 5m + curl retries + margin), or lower the inner `kubectl wait --timeout` to 5m so its failure surfaces with a targeted error before the outer kill.

issues_found.title: Unauthenticated Docker Hub HelmRepository is subject to anonymous pull rate limits
issues_found.description: `url: oci://docker.io/envoyproxy` pulls the Envoy Gateway chart anonymously. Docker Hub throttles anonymous pulls to 100/6h per source IP; CI runners behind shared NAT can hit the limit on busy days, surfacing as `TOOMANYREQUESTS` from source-controller and turning the envoy-gateway HelmRelease wait (Phase 3 in deploy-infra.sh) into a flake. The DECISION block already acknowledges the chart is not yet mirrored into c5c3-charts.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Either (a) mirror the chart into the existing `oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts` source to align with the other addon charts, (b) optionally authenticate via a `secretRef` when DOCKERHUB_USER is available, or (c) document the rate-limit risk in the DECISION block so a future flake lands on the right root cause.

issues_found.title: HelmRepository and HelmRelease share the name `envoy-gateway`
issues_found.description: `HelmRepository/envoy-gateway` (flux-system ns) and `HelmRelease/envoy-gateway` (envoy-gateway-system ns) share a name. Different namespaces and kinds keep the API safe, but `kubectl get` output and flux log lines become ambiguous ("envoy-gateway not ready" could mean the repo OR the release).
issues_found.suggested_fix: Rename the repository to `envoy-gateway-charts` (or similar) so logs disambiguate repo vs. release. Optional — not load-bearing.

suggested_improvements: Gateway-quick-start suite could additionally assert Gateway's `.status.listeners[name=https].attachedRoutes == 1` — this would catch the HTTPRoute-conflict race flagged above while the conflict symptom is still "no listener binding" rather than "timeout waiting for HTTPRouteReady".

deploy-infra.sh's wait_for_gateway_programmed currently dumps logs for every pod in envoy-gateway-system without filtering by `--since`. On long runs the `--tail=200` may already exclude the relevant crash frame. Consider `--since=10m` or similar to keep diagnostics focused.

Consider adding a `make quick-start` or similar one-shot target that chains `make deploy-infra` + keystone build/load + `kubectl apply -f keystone.yaml` from the Quick Start so the curl-accessible endpoint can be reached from a single invocation (not a blocker, but would close the loop on the "Quick Start" narrative CC-0088 sells).
next_steps: Delete or rewrite the two `# Reviewer: please verify` comments in deploy/kind/base/envoy-gateway.yaml:36 and :47 before merge.
Decide on the HTTPRoute-collision mitigation for the smoke + keystone-suite pair — simplest fix is `path: /v3` in the smoke fixture.
Either raise the smoke suite's chainsaw `timeouts.exec/script.timeout` to ≥ 9m or lower the inner `kubectl wait --timeout=8m` to 5m so timeout diagnostics fire before the chainsaw kill.
Merge after the above three low-cost adjustments; no blocker-level rework required.
detected_patterns: Overlay gating: chainsaw metadata.labels.overlay=kind selector + runtime presence probe (from tests/e2e/infrastructure/flux-web-health/chainsaw-test.yaml)
JSONPath-style condition assertion: `(conditions[?type == 'X'])` projection in chainsaw (from tests/e2e/keystone/httproute/chainsaw-test.yaml)
Stub-kubectl + source-and-invoke unit-test pattern for hack/deploy-infra.sh helpers (from tests/unit/hack/deploy_infra_reconcile_sources_test.sh)
Renovate customManager regex with automerge minor/patch + majors disabled + minimumReleaseAge (from renovate.json flux-operator + flux-web-chart rules)
SPDX header + Feature: CC-XXXX, REQ-N footer on every new file (project-wide convention)
DECISION/Ambiguity/Chose/Reason comment block in manifests (from tests/e2e/chainsaw-config.yaml:8-12 header style)
production-posture git-diff guard vs origin/main (unit test uses the same `deploy/flux-system/ untouched` invariant the feature ticket calls out as REQ-012)

metadata.feature_title: Expose Keystone API via Gateway API with Envoy and nip.io
metadata.scope_note: Strictly in-diff review. Unchanged files referenced only as context for pattern matching (e.g. flux-web-health chainsaw suite, httproute chainsaw suite, existing renovate entries).
